title: Fee Calculation
description: Bitcoin transaction fees are the difference between total input value and total output value. To reason about whether a fee is high or low in practice, learners also need to relate that absolute fee to transaction weight and virtual bytes.

resources.title: Decoding Bitcoin: Fee Calculation
resources.notes: Beginner-friendly walkthrough of fee = inputs minus outputs, feerates, minimum relay fees, and fee-bumping context.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Transactions
resources.notes: Canonical transaction accounting background for inputs, outputs, and miner fee collection.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad technical reference for transaction construction, feerates, and blockspace pricing tradeoffs.
